commit.message:
fix: Telegram bot improvements — inspect, dedup, context (#54)

* fix: handle Anthropic tool_use finish_reason in agent loop

The agent loop checked for finish_reason == "tool_calls" (OpenAI
format) but Anthropic returns "tool_use". This caused the loop to
break after round 1 without processing tool results, producing
empty responses ("no response generated").

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add /inspect command to Telegram bot

Shows tool actions, sources, and timing from the last query.
Also captures statuses, sources, and perf_trace events from
the chat SSE stream for inspection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: prevent duplicate Telegram messages from Claude Code sessions

[NOTIFY] messages were sent during assistant events AND re-extracted
from the result event, causing duplicates. Now skips re-extraction
when notifications were already sent during the session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: detect imperative follow-ups for conversation context retention

"look in my email", "check my calendar", "search drive for it" etc.
were not recognized as follow-ups, so the previous question's context
was lost. Added regex pattern for imperative verbs + data source nouns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add tests for PR review findings; strip PII from /inspect

- Add unit tests for expand_followup_query imperative patterns
- Add tests for orchestrator notification dedup (notifications_sent > 0)
- Move _IMPERATIVE_FOLLOWUP_RE to module level per convention
- Strip tool input args from /inspect output (show tool name only)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Try getTranscript() first, fall back to captionUrl for client fetch

getTranscript() uses Innertube JSON API (not CDN) so avoids IP blocking.
Added detailed logging. useSummarize handles both response shapes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Codex + ElevenLabs services, fix Claude utilization parsing, add Sonnet bar

- Fix critical bug: Claude OAuth API returns utilization as percentage (0-100),
  not fraction (0.0-1.0). Divide by 100 to normalize.
- Fix response parsing: API returns usage data at top level, not wrapped in
  a `data` field. Remove wrapper struct.
- Add Codex service: reads OAuth from ~/.codex/auth.json, calls
  chatgpt.com/backend-api/wham/usage with rate limit headers
- Add ElevenLabs service: API key based, calls
  api.elevenlabs.io/v1/user/subscription for character quota
- Add Grok API key validation via api.x.ai/v1/models
- Remove Gemini (no desktop app, no simple API endpoint)
- Add Sonnet-only 7-day utilization bar to QuotaDisplay
- Add first-class extra credits fields (no more JSON parsing)
- Auto-fetch real usage for all services with session/API access

Fix score ring font size on performance page (#117)

## Summary
- Fixed score ring font size calculation that used `rem` instead of
`px`, causing numbers to render at ~174px and overflow their containers
- Changed `size * 0.17 + 'rem'` to `size * 0.3 + 'px'` for correct
sizing (19.2px for 64px rings)

## Test plan
- [ ] Run a PageSpeed test on the performance page and verify score
numbers fit inside the ring circles
- [ ] Check both "Your Site" and "Our Portfolio Avg" columns render
correctly

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

fix(e2e): F1e focus restore — focus row before modal open for Radix restore

The modal was opened via keyboard shortcut with no trigger button,
so Radix Dialog restored focus to body (invisible to :focus locator).
Fix: focus a finding row before opening modal so Radix restores to it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Migrate to AGP 9.1.0 and update dependencies (#447)

* Update version catalog and add CLAUDE.md for project guidance

* Migrate to AGP 9.1.0 and update dependencies

Upgrade AGP 8.13.0 → 9.1.0 with coordinated dependency updates:
Hilt/Dagger 2.59.2, Kotlin 2.3.10, KSP 2.3.6, triplet-play 4.0.0,
Compose BOM 2026.02.01, Gradle wrapper 9.4.0.

Adapt convention plugins for AGP 9 breaking changes: remove
CommonExtension type parameters, convert DSL lambda blocks to direct
property access, switch to built-in Kotlin (remove kotlin-android
plugin), fix LibraryExtension import, and remove jetifier property.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Replace detektMain with detekt in CI and scripts

AGP 9 built-in Kotlin changes source set registration, so detekt
no longer creates per-source-set tasks like detektMain. Use the
aggregate detekt task instead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Comment out detektMain until detekt supports AGP 9 built-in Kotlin

detektMain is not available with AGP 9's built-in Kotlin source set
registration. Commented out with TODO linking to detekt/detekt#8320.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update CLAUDE.md for AGP 9 migration

Update Gradle/API versions, replace pre-migration upgrade notes with
post-migration convention plugin guidance (built-in Kotlin, CommonExtension
changes, detektMain status).

commit.message:
fix(ceo-review): correct valid agent names in system prompt

CEO was generating auto-sprints with agent="agents" and agent="infra"
(both invalid), causing every auto-generated task to immediately fail
with "Agent not found" and cascade-skip all dependents.

HEALTH-001 previously fixed agents/ceo/prompt.md but missed the
hardcoded system prompt in scripts/run-ceo-review.ts.

Valid agent names now match what the orchestrator actually loads from
the agents/ directory (minus skipped: ceo, supervisor, cto, cmo).

commit.message:
Force webpack build to fix API route 404s on Vercel

Vercel's @vercel/next adapter does not correctly process Next.js 16
Turbopack production build output for API routes — they are silently
excluded from the routing table and return static 404 HTML.

Switching to --webpack ensures the build output format that Vercel's
adapter understands, so all API routes are deployed as Node.js serverless
functions. Also restores cacheComponents + 'use cache' directives which
are the recommended caching API in Next.js 16.

[CI] Narrow KB cache key to avoid unnecessary 14-min rebuilds (#443)

Replace the broad `datus/storage/**/*.py` glob with specific paths that
actually affect KB construction output. This prevents unrelated storage
changes (scoped_filter, lancedb_conditions, cache, etc.) from
invalidating the ~14-minute LLM-dependent KB build.

Included paths:
- build_scripts/build_test_data.sh, tests/conf/agent.yml, sample_data/**
- datus/storage/{schema_metadata,reference_sql,ext_knowledge,metric,
  semantic_model,subject_tree}/**
- datus/storage/base.py, embedding_models.py, fastembed_embeddings.py
- datus/agent/node/{sql_summary,gen_ext_knowledge,gen_metrics,
  gen_semantic_model}_agentic_node.py
- datus/prompts/prompt_templates/*.j2

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: replace Unicode symbol icons with SVGs (#375) (#383)

* fix: replace Unicode symbol icons with SVGs to prevent tofu squares (#375)

Unicode characters like ✎ ✗ ☰ − render as blank squares on devices
without those glyphs in their font. Replace with SVG icons from
qrc:/icons/ which render identically on all platforms.

Changes:
- ProfileSelectorPage: Edit/Delete/Remove menu items use SVG icons
- ShotHistoryPage: Saved button uses list.svg icon
- Added minus.svg and trash.svg to icon set
- CLAUDE.md: Added rules against Unicode symbols as icons and
  overriding FINAL properties on Qt types

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: replace all risky Unicode symbols with SVG icons across app

Comprehensive replacement of Unicode characters that render as tofu
squares on devices without the right font glyphs:

- ✓ checkmarks → tick.svg (15 instances across 12 files)
- ✕ X marks → cross.svg (6 instances across 4 files)
- ★☆ stars → star.svg/star-outline.svg (6 instances)
- ⋮ vertical ellipsis → more-vertical.svg (1 instance)
- ⚠ warning → warning.svg (1 instance)
- ≡ hamburger → list.svg (1 instance)

Added warning.svg and more-vertical.svg to icon set.

Safe Unicode characters (°, ·, —, →, ×, ▼, ▲) left unchanged as they
have near-universal font support.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: add CLAUDE.md rules for Unicode symbols and FINAL properties

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: generate AndroidManifest.xml from template at build time

AndroidManifest.xml was checked in with hardcoded versionCode and
versionName that would drift from CMakeLists.txt and versioncode.txt,
causing a perpetually dirty working tree after builds.

Now generated from AndroidManifest.xml.in by ApplyVersionCode.cmake,
matching the existing pattern for version.h and version.iss. Both
versionCode and versionName come from their single source of truth.

Changes:
- Added android/AndroidManifest.xml.in template with @placeholders@
- Updated ApplyVersionCode.cmake to use configure_file()
- Removed manifest sed from Android CI (only versioncode.txt committed)
- Gitignored the generated AndroidManifest.xml
- Updated CLAUDE.md versioning docs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: make MAX_POINTS public to fix build error

Moving MAX_POINTS from private to public in FastLineRenderer so it can
be accessed from file scope in fastlinerenderer.cpp (needed after PR #381
replaced the duplicate STRIP_MAX_POINTS constant).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add cmake warning for missing manifest template + restore color-coded import icons

- ApplyVersionCode.cmake now warns when AndroidManifest.xml.in is missing
  instead of silently skipping manifest generation
- ProfileImportPage status icons now use colored circle backgrounds
  (primary/success/warning) to preserve semantic distinction lost when
  switching from colored Unicode text to white SVGs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: restore original icon colors lost in Unicode→SVG migration

Use Button with icon.source + icon.color to tint white SVGs to their
original semantic colors (primaryColor, successColor, warningButtonColor).
Affects dropdown checkmarks, completion overlay, import status icons,
success badges, and sort picker checkmarks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add ColoredIcon component and use theme colors for all SVG icons

Create ColoredIcon.qml — a display-only tinted SVG icon that uses
Button's internal IconLabel for icon.color tinting. Replace all
opacity-hack and raw-Button workarounds with ColoredIcon.

This ensures icons respect user-customizable theme colors
(textSecondaryColor, primaryColor, successColor, etc.) instead of
using hardcoded white with opacity.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: add icon.source support to AccessibleButton

Update AccessibleButton contentItem to handle both icon+text and
text-only rendering via a Row with Image + Text. This eliminates
verbose contentItem overrides at call sites.

Simplify 3 ShotHistoryPage buttons from ~15-line contentItem
overrides to single icon.source property.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: ColoredIcon opacity, search clear button, and i18n

- ColoredIcon: remove enabled:false which caused Material style to
  render all icons at 38% opacity. Use absorbing MouseArea instead.
- ShotHistoryPage: change search clear from Text to Item with explicit
  size so the icon and MouseArea are visible/functional.
- VisualizerMultiImportPage: wrap 2 hardcoded English legend strings
  with TranslationManager.translate().

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore(docs): architecture review, competitive landscape, and naming research (#44)

* chore(arch): draft architecture review with 3 variant proposals

Four independent review agents analyzed the codebase from server-side,
client-side, domain model, and DevOps perspectives. Consolidated findings
into 19 weaknesses (5 critical, 10 high, 19 medium) and the aspiration-vs-
reality gap between ARCHITECTURE.md claims and implementation.

Proposes 3 architectural directions:
- Variant A: Harden the Foundation (low risk, 4-6 cycles)
- Variant B: Event-Driven Pipeline (medium risk, 6-9 cycles)
- Variant C: Modular Platform (high risk, 8-12 cycles)

Recommends: Start A, plan for B, defer C.

Co-authored-by: Simon <thiscantbeserious@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore(arch): v2 — challenge vision itself, forward-looking variants

Expanded architecture review with three additional strategic review agents:
- Vision Skeptic: demand validation, behavioral assumptions, feedback loop
  validity, temporal relevance, asciicast lock-in, white-label ambition
- Alignment Analyst: path to curation/retrieval/collaboration, MVP sequence
  problem, missing architectural concepts, bounded context validity
- Strategist: ecosystem positioning, format evolution, threats, opportunities

Key additions:
- Part 1 now challenges the vision thesis directly (behavioral bet,
  feedback loop gap, competitive landscape, format lock-in)
- Bounded context revision: Cache/Index/Transform reclassified, Curation
  added as missing core context
- Threat analysis with existential risks and competitor response
- 3 variants reframed as strategic directions, not bug-fix plans:
  A: Curation-First (validate the thesis fast)
  B: Agent Work Reviewer (multi-format platform play)
  C: Refinement Engine (deep intelligence on one ecosystem)
- Recommendation: Variant A with C elements — ship the loop first

Co-authored-by: Simon <thiscantbeserious@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore(arch): replace all ASCII diagrams with Mermaid

Converted 6 ASCII art diagrams to Mermaid flowcharts:
- Vision claimed loop (Part 1.1)
- Variant A roadmap comparison
- Variant B state transition comparison
- Variant B architecture diagram (ingest layer)
- Variant C state transition comparison
- Variant C learning loop
- Recommended sequence (Part 6)

All detail preserved. No box-drawing characters remain.

Co-authored-by: Simon <thiscantbeserious@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore(arch): naming research — Erika expansion and RAGTS retirement

Research document covering:
- Proposed expansion: Engine for Reinforcement & Interactive Knowledge
  Alignment — each letter maps to a vision concept
- RLHF parallel: 'Reinforcement' + 'Knowledge Alignment' positions
  Erika in the human feedback discourse
- Etymology: Old Norse 'sole ruler', German typewriter heritage,
  botanical hardiness
- Conflict analysis: ERIKA Enterprise (RTOS) is different domain,
  other Erikas negligible, package registries mostly available
- RAGTS retirement case: format lock-in ('Terminal Sessions'),
  RAG/TS confusion, dual-name friction, already the minority identity
- SEO analysis, tagline proposals, usage guidelines
- 6 alternative expansions evaluated and rejected

Co-authored-by: Simon <thiscantbeserious@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore(arch): competitive landscape — 12 competitors rated on likelihood + uniqueness

Major findings:
- Humanloop is DEAD (acqui-hired by Anthropic, Aug 2025)
- 3 direct open-source competitors emerged in 2026:
  AgentReplay (Rust, AGPL, MCP+OTel+evaluators — most dangerous),
  AgentsView (Go, single binary, 327★), AgentLogs (TS, team features)
- 4 adjacent open-source platforms: Langfuse (MIT, most complete),
  Phoenix/Arize, Helicone, Weave
- 4 CLI tools: cass, fast-resume, engram, agf
- asciicast is NOT the lingua franca — agent-native JSONL and OTel are
- Recommended adapter priority: asciicast → Claude Code JSONL → OTel → MCP
- Erika's defensible position: the only platform combining terminal-native
  rendering + human curation + MCP retrieval + self-hosted web + adapters

Co-authored-by: Simon <thiscantbeserious@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore(docs): fix AgentReplay claims, verify competitor data, replace ASCII with markdown/mermaid

- AgentReplay: correct "native desktop app" to "local-first CLI tool", adjust ratings
- AgentsView: update stars 327→379
- Langfuse: update stars 8k→22.9k
- Phoenix: update stars 8.5k→8.8k
- agf: correct agent count to 5, remove unverified git branch tracking
- Replace ASCII comparison tables with markdown tables
- Replace ASCII box diagrams with Mermaid flowcharts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore(docs): move architecture review artifacts to .research/step1/

commit.message:
API whitelist, collapsible completed quests, product field, multi-category, human-input flag

- API whitelist: Support multiple keys via API_KEYS (comma-separated) env var
  Backward compatible: API_KEY still works. Each agent can have its own key.
- Multi-category: categories[] array field replaces single category string
  Old quests with category string auto-normalized to [category] on load
  category param still accepted for backward compat (converted to array)
- Human input flag: humanInputRequired boolean on quests
  Dashboard shows orange "Needs Leon" badge on quest cards
  Helps agents know: do not claim, requires human involvement
- Completed quests: each row is now individually collapsible
  Collapsed: title + completedBy + time. Expanded: description, categories, product, completedAt
- Product field: optional product enum on quests (Dashboard, Companion App, Infrastructure, Other)
  Shown as italic badge on quest cards next to category badges
- API docs updated for all new fields (Quest schema, createQuest endpoint)
- docker-compose.yml updated with API_KEYS example

commit.message:
fix(install): comprehensive Windows/WSL installation chain hardening

Fixes all identified blockers in the WSL install chain:

install.sh:
- Add run_pkg() helper: uses direct commands when root (Alpine WSL),
  falls back to sudo when available. Fixes "sudo: not found" on Alpine.
- Auto-install curl if missing (pre-flight check)
- Detect musl-based systems (Alpine) and auto-install gcompat + libstdc++
  for glibc binary compatibility
- Warn users about potential Alpine incompatibility

install.ps1:
- Show install.sh output (removed stderr suppression) so users can
  see tmux/binary installation progress and errors
- Add post-install verification: check agent-hand and tmux are in PATH,
  attempt tmux install if missing
- Fix shim to use login shell (bash -lc) so ~/.local/bin PATH additions
  from install.sh are picked up at runtime
- Also auto-install curl alongside bash in the fallback step

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Deepgram Nova-2 STT provider — reliable paid STT replacing Chrome Web Speech

- Add DeepgramSTT.js client provider (MediaRecorder + VAD → /api/stt/deepgram)
- Add /api/stt/deepgram server endpoint (Deepgram Nova-2 REST API)
- Profile-driven STT selection: deepgram | groq | webspeech
- Default profile switched to deepgram
- Fix [CANVAS_URL:] tag leaking into TTS audio
- Strengthen English-only voice instructions

commit.message:
feat: add persistent volume support for Railway deployment

- start.sh: seeds /data volume with 123 items + 174 photos on first run
- Dockerfile: uses start.sh as entrypoint, creates /data/uploads fallback
- railway.toml: build/deploy config with health check

Set env vars in Railway: UPLOAD_DIR=/data/uploads, DB_FILE=/data/database.json
Mount a Railway Volume at /data to persist data across deploys.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

목표 페이지 로딩 속도 개선 (Server Component 전환)

- OKR 페이지를 Client → Server Component로 전환하여
  서버에서 직접 Prisma 쿼리 실행 (API 왕복 제거)
- 기존: JS로드 → hydrate → useEffect → fetch API → auth → DB → 렌더
- 개선: 서버 auth + DB 쿼리 → HTML에 데이터 포함 → 즉시 렌더
- loading.tsx 추가로 로딩 중 스켈레톤 UI 즉시 표시
- OKRClient 컴포넌트 분리 (목표 추가/삭제 등 상호작용 유지)
